title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 30th January 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Hold from IPO; sees upside to Rs 3,500-3,600
recommendations.detail: Advised a viewer holding since the IPO to hold. MTAR touched a 52-week high of 2,844 and trades around 2,805-2,810 after an excellent consolidated result (~100% jump). He feels it can go up to Rs 3,500-3,600 thanks to its Bloom-Energy-type product and data-centre supplies, and called it the best of the Chennai recommendations.

recommendations.note: Budget-related micro-cap; slowly accumulate, average after results
recommendations.detail: A viewer bought at Rs 267 on Muhurat day; it has fallen to Rs 170. He called it a Budget-related micro-cap to slowly accumulate over the long term. Advised waiting for this quarter's results and management guidance; if numbers come better, then average.

recommendations.note: Hold; IPO froth cooled, watch NPA recovery
recommendations.detail: A viewer is at a ~29% loss. He called it a decent company whose speculative IPO-time activity has cooled off. A recent rise in NPAs is a concern and the recovery needs watching, so he advised holding for now.

recommendations.note: Hold; demerger of medical-devices business could add momentum
recommendations.detail: A viewer is at a ~42% loss. Given the big correction in mid- and small-cap stocks, he advised holding. Their medical-devices business is heading for a listing/demerger, and after that news there is a chance of some momentum in the stock.

recommendations.note: Decide only after the upcoming result
recommendations.detail: A viewer asked whether to hold or sell. He noted the company did a large equity expansion (~Rs 200 crore) that is hard to service, so EPS only becomes market-digestible with a lot of effort. They disappointed at the last result; he advised taking a decision only after seeing the upcoming result.
